title: Behavior for absolute / out-of-tree --artifact paths on stage end (#350)
context: stage end --artifact currently join()s the path onto projectRoot, so an absolute or out-of-tree path silently becomes a non-existent in-repo path, is hashed as 'absent', and the stage folds to inconclusive while the CLI still prints recorded:true. We must stop the silent-accept. Anti-spoofing (a genuinely-missing in-tree file still records 'absent') must NOT weaken. Question: what should an absolute/out-of-tree path do?

options.label: Reject ALL absolute paths with a clear error + non-zero exit (artifact must be a path inside the project)
Accept an absolute path when it resolves to a real file INSIDE the project root (normalize to relative); reject only genuinely out-of-tree paths
Accept in-tree absolute, reject out-of-tree, and always fail loudly rather than recording a false 'absent'

rationale: Human chose: accept absolute paths that resolve inside project root (normalize to relative), reject genuinely out-of-tree paths with a clear error + non-zero exit. Anti-spoof for missing in-tree files unchanged.
